title: Paldean Tauros (Blaze) Battle Guide
summary: A [Fighting]/[Fire] physical attacker with [intimidate] — [raging-bull] is a signature [Fire] STAB that also shatters screens.
weaknesses: No sun abuse without external setup
bodyMarkdown:
## Overview
Paldean Tauros (Blaze Breed) adds [Fire] STAB on top of the Combat Breed's [Fighting] identity. Its signature move [raging-bull] becomes [Fire]-typed, gains a respectable BP, and breaks opposing screens — making it a highly flexible anti-stall attacker. [intimidate] support and 110/100 offensive profile complete the toolkit.

## Strengths
[raging-bull] is the unique feature — a signature move that changes type by breed and additionally shatters [light-screen] and [reflect]. This turns Blaze Breed Tauros into a counter to screen-based stall teams. [Fighting]/[Fire] dual STAB is an excellent offensive combination that covers [Steel], [Ice], [Dark], [Bug], and [Grass] at a minimum.

## Weaknesses & Answers
[Water], [Ground], [Flying], and [Psychic] are all active weaknesses, and [Water] attackers threaten outright KOs. Bulk is average, so predicting around weaknesses matters.

## Recommended Moveset
[raging-bull] is the signature [Fire] STAB. [close-combat] is the [Fighting] STAB. [flare-blitz] is an alternative to [raging-bull] for more raw damage but loses the screen-break utility. [protect] is mandatory.

## Item · Ability · Tera
[life-orb] for offensive flexibility. [choice-band] for maximum damage. [intimidate] is locked. Tera [Fire] stacks [raging-bull] and [flare-blitz], Tera [Fighting] stacks [close-combat].

## Teammates & Synergy
Cover [Water] and [Ground] with [Electric] and [Grass] partners. [Dark] and [Bug] cover [Psychic]. [tailwind] extends the sweeping window.

## Play Patterns
Lead to trigger [intimidate], then click [raging-bull] against screen leads or [close-combat] into neutral targets. Flex [flare-blitz] when screen-breaking isn't needed. Switch out against [Water] attackers.
